Ad Text:
The Servcorp vision is to empower our clients with market-leading Information and Communication Technology (ICT) products and services that provide their businesses with a competitive advantage in an experience that is smart, simple, and consistent.




Servcorp’s ICT mission is to deliver on the following:

To evolve our products and services to allow Servcorp clients to establish a competitive market edge;

Innovate with purpose-built technology that underpins our key product and service offerings;

Enhance our client experiences stemming from new capabilities, insights, and systems;

Provide a global IT team to support the business 24 hours a day, seven days a week, delivering a stable and reliable service to clients; and

Promote ongoing exposure to innovative technology to ensure Servcorp, and subsequently, Servcorp’s clients, remain ahead of the curve.




Overall responsibility

The Data & AI Analyst role is a key component in Servcorp Technology (ServTech) achieving this mission. The role is responsible for supporting internal data-driven decision-making processes by leveraging artificial intelligence and machine learning techniques. This role involves analyzing complex datasets, generating insights, and working closely with internal teams to enhance operational efficiency and business intelligence.

Servcorp is the technology leader in our industry!




Position Overview

Manage and maintain Servcorp’s large datasets.

Analyze and interpret large datasets to generate AI-driven insights.

Develop and implement machine learning models for business optimization.

Automate data processing and reporting workflows.

Collaborate with internal teams to integrate AI into decision-making processes.

Ensure data accuracy, security, and compliance with governance policies.

Stay updated with the latest advancements in AI, data analytics, and industry trends.

To collaborate with teams across the business to maintain an AI strategy that aligns with business requirements.




Essential Job Functions

Support internal teams with data analysis, visualization, and AI-driven insights.

Collect, clean, and preprocess internal datasets for reporting and decision-making.

Develop and implement AI-driven models to optimize business operations.

Conduct exploratory data analysis to uncover patterns and trends.

Automate internal data processing and reporting workflows.

Assist in the development and monitoring of AI-powered business solutions.

Collaborate with IT, engineering, and business teams to align AI strategies with organizational goals.

Ensure compliance with internal data governance policies and security standards.

Stay updated with advancements in AI and data analytics relevant to the company’s needs.




Key Performance Indicators

Accuracy and efficiency of AI-driven insights and recommendations.

Improvement in business processes through AI and data analytics.

Reduction in manual data processing time via automation initiatives.
Adoption rate of AI-driven solutions across internal departments.

Timeliness and quality of data reporting and visualization.

Successful deployment and maintenance of AI models in production.

Internal stakeholder satisfaction with data and AI services.

Compliance with internal data governance and security protocols.




Requirements

Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Data Science, Computer Science, Statistics, Mathematics, or a related field.

Experience in data analysis, machine learning, or AI-driven analytics.

Strong relationship and communication skills, excellent written, verbal and presentation skills with the ability to communicate and simplify complex technical ideas/solutions.

Knowledge of technological trends to help build strategy.

Proficiency in programming languages.

Proficiency in data visualization tools (Power BI etc.).

Exposure to data warehousing infrastructure in Microsoft Azure and Fabric.

Strong understanding of statistical analysis and machine learning techniques.

Familiarity with AI frameworks.

Experience with internal data management and business intelligence tools.

Excellent problem-solving and critical-thinking skills.

Technical ability to be able to liaise seamlessly with a variety of stakeholders including clients, engineers and developers.

Self-motivated to learn about new emerging technologies and new methodologies.

Proactive problem solver.

Attention to detail.

Company Description


Forward with Cuscal 

At Cuscal, we’re not just shaping the future of payments - we’re pioneering it. From launching Australia’s first ATM to being the first certified NPP PayTo Payer and Initiator in 2022, we’ve spent over 59 years building solutions that millions of Australians rely on every day.  

Now, we’re preparing for what’s next, and we want you to be part of it. 

Join us as an Environment Automation Engineer in our Engineering Practice team and help us deliver ground-breaking solutions that push the boundaries of what’s possible. 

Job Description


The Environment Automation Engineer is responsible for designing and delivering automated environment management solutions that support development, testing, and production teams. Operating within the Engineering Practices function, the role enables squads and domains to efficiently provision, manage, and maintain secure, reliable environments aligned with organisational standards.

The role focuses on reducing manual effort through automation, optimising tools and workflows, and promoting self‑service and operational resilience. In addition, the Environment Automation Engineer provides hands‑on support, guidance, and capability uplift to teams, enabling sustainable, high‑quality environment management practices and long‑term operational excellence.

Qualifications


What you’ll bring 

To succeed in this role, you’ll have:

Experience with scripting (Bash, PowerShell, Python) for automation
Database experience (SQL)
Working knowledge of YAML, JSON, and XML
Terraform and Infrastructure‑as‑Code experience in AWS
Hands-on with CI/CD tools such as Bamboo and GitHub Actions
Solid understanding of networking (DNS, TCP/IP, load balancing) and security fundamental
Strong knowledge of system design & integration
Experience with monitoring tools such as Control-M, Splunk, CloudWatch, or BMC for tracking system performance, troubleshooting and the creation of dashboards for actionable insights.
Hands on experience in delivering solutions
Defining and building automation patterns
Supporting developers in design and build
Supporting testers in coverage and execution
Resolving roadblocks
Good problem-solving skills
Identifying key delivery risks
Sequencing of tasks

Additional Information

Here, you’ll deliver or support interesting, ground-breaking work that has real impact - on Australia’s financial services sector and the millions of customers it serves. You’ll innovate alongside skilled, smart, connected teams. And you’ll build an impressive, fulfilling career that continues to grow.   

You’ll also enjoy a range of benefits, including:

We celebrate success: Our IGNITE program recognises individual and team achievements.
Wellbeing focus: We support your physical, mental, and financial health with holistic initiatives and access to discounts via ‘Cuscal Advantage.

Join us and reimagine the future
